claim: Deal with model rot (outdated training data) by serving fresh, up-to-date context via markdown documentation and RAG tools that agents can access on demand
actionable: Maintain fresh markdown documentation alongside your product; allow agents to select and load context based on detected integrations or use cases.
source_title: LLM codegen fails and how to stop 'em — Danilo Campos, PostHog

claim: Use 'model airplanes' — thin simulacrum projects with correct architecture but minimal functionality — to teach agents the shape of correct integrations
actionable: Maintain simplified reference projects for each integration pattern (e.g., auth flows, API setups) without production complexity; include them in agent context.
source_title: LLM codegen fails and how to stop 'em — Danilo Campos, PostHog

claim: Breadcrumb the agent with incremental steps instead of prescribing exact paths: ask it to find impactful files first, then list events, then implement details
actionable: Break complex agent tasks into discovery, ideation, and execution phases; don't tell the agent the full destination upfront to avoid rushed final steps.
source_title: LLM codegen fails and how to stop 'em — Danilo Campos, PostHog

claim: Use inference-time interrogation: ask the agent at the end of runs what you could have done better to set it up for success to catch instruction gaps
actionable: Add a post-execution hook that asks agents: What could we have done better? Use their feedback to find missing permissions, contradictory directives, or wrong language instructions.
source_title: LLM codegen fails and how to stop 'em — Danilo Campos, PostHog

claim: Lock down tool access for sensitive operations: check presence of keys without reading ENV contents; write new values without exposing secrets to logs
actionable: For ENV file operations, expose only narrow tools (check_key_exists, write_key_value) that don't touch actual values; never log or send secrets to inference.
source_title: LLM codegen fails and how to stop 'em — Danilo Campos, PostHog

claim: Focus on prose and markdown documentation rather than scaffolded code; good prose today improves with better models tomorrow, while code depreciates
actionable: Invest in clear, comprehensive markdown instructions and examples for agents; these are stable assets that benefit from future model improvements.
source_title: LLM codegen fails and how to stop 'em — Danilo Campos, PostHog
